ralph-loop-parameters.on-unrecoverable: Write JSONL event + create GitHub issue + exit 0 (never hang)

on-success.action: Close GitHub issue #744 with VRAM measurement + run summary
Create PR test/mambavesselnet → main via gh pr create

on-failure.action: Write FAILURE event to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl (always before exit)
Create GitHub issue with diagnosis category + log excerpts
Exit cleanly with code 0 (never hang, never wait for user)

task.name: Verify RunPod enabled + GPU available

commands.cmd: uv run sky check runpod 2>&1 | grep -i enabled || (echo "FATAL: RunPod not enabled" && exit 1)
uv run sky status 2>&1 | head -15 # Show controller state

task.acceptance: RunPod: enabled. Controller running at EU-CZ-1 (expected from prior session).

task.name: GHCR auth — login before manifest check and push
task.description: FIX FOR B1: GHCR package is private. RunPod cannot pull private GHCR without credentials. Options (in priority order): Option A (RECOMMENDED): Make GHCR package public — GitHub → Packages → minivess-base → Settings → Public Option B: Pass docker creds via SkyPilot --docker-login flag in Makefile This task does the login needed for docker manifest inspect in T0.4. The GHCR package must be verified as public before cloud launch (T3.1).

commands.cmd: echo "${GITHUB_TOKEN}" | docker login ghcr.io -u "${GHCR_USERNAME}" --password-stdin 2>&1 \ || (echo "FATAL: GHCR login failed — check GITHUB_TOKEN in .env" && exit 1)

# Verify GHCR package visibility (must be public for RunPod pull without auth) VISIBILITY=$(gh api \ repos/petteriteikari/minivess-mlops/packages?package_type=container \ 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import json,sys pkgs = json.load(sys.stdin) if sys.stdin.readable() else [] for p in pkgs if isinstance(pkgs, list) else []: if 'minivess-base' in p.get('name', ''): print(p.get('visibility', 'unknown')) " 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown") echo "GHCR package visibility: $VISIBILITY" if [ "$VISIBILITY" = "private" ]; then echo "WARNING: GHCR package is private. RunPod will fail docker pull." echo "Fix: GitHub → Packages → minivess-base → Settings → Make Public" echo "Or set USE_PUBLIC_GHCR=0 and pass --docker-login in Makefile." exit 1 fi

task.acceptance: GHCR login OK. Package visibility = public (or token-based pull configured in Makefile).
task.failure-action: Make package public: gh api -X PATCH repos/petteriteikari/minivess-mlops/packages/container/minivess-base \ -f visibility=public Then re-run T0.2.

task.name: Create Network Volume if missing
task.description: FIX FOR B2: sky storage ls returns empty — Network Volume must be created before launch. smoke_test_mamba.yaml setup hard-gates on /opt/vol existing. If volume doesn't exist, the pod starts but /opt/vol is not mounted → FATAL in setup. This task creates the volume if missing (idempotent — safe to run even if exists).

commands.cmd: VOLUMES=$(uv run sky storage ls 2>&1) echo "Current volumes: $VOLUMES" if echo "$VOLUMES" | grep -q "minivess-dev"; then echo "OK: Network Volume minivess-dev exists" else echo "Creating Network Volume minivess-dev (50 GB, EU-RO-1)..." if [ -f "deployment/skypilot/minivess-dev-volume.yaml" ]; then uv run sky storage apply deployment/skypilot/minivess-dev-volume.yaml 2>&1 else # Inline volume creation if YAML missing uv run sky storage create minivess-dev --cloud runpod --size 50 --region EU-RO-1 2>&1 fi uv run sky storage ls 2>&1 | grep minivess-dev \ || (echo "FATAL: Volume creation failed" && exit 1) fi

task.acceptance: sky storage ls shows minivess-dev volume (50 GB, EU-RO-1).

task.name: Check if mamba Docker image already exists (skip P1 if yes)

commands.cmd: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest 2>&1 | head -3

task.decision: If manifest returns valid JSON: IMAGE_EXISTS=true → SKIP P1 (Docker build not needed). If "manifest unknown": IMAGE_EXISTS=false → PROCEED to P1. Set IMAGE_EXISTS flag for P1 gate condition.

task.name: Stop jobs controller before Docker build (cost save)
task.description: FIX: Stop the already-running controller before 45-min Docker build to save $0.13. Controller will be auto-restarted when needed by sky jobs launch in P3. Only stop if IMAGE_EXISTS=false (build is needed).

commands.cmd: if [ "${IMAGE_EXISTS:-false}" = "false" ]; then echo "Stopping jobs controller to save $0.13 during build..." uv run sky down sky-jobs-controller-aec1627c -y 2>&1 || true else echo "Image already exists — keeping controller, skipping build" fi

task.note: Controller auto-restarts on next sky jobs launch (P3.T3.1). No manual restart needed.

task.name: Build :mamba-latest image + verify mamba-ssm survives COPY
task.description: FIX FOR B5: After build, MUST verify mamba-ssm .so files survived builder→runner COPY. Dockerfile.base copies /app/.venv from builder. If mamba-ssm CUDA extensions are compiled to a cache dir outside .venv, the runner stage won't have them. The verification docker run catches this before wasting $0.12+ on a cloud job.

commands.cmd: make build-mamba 2>&1 | tee /tmp/docker-build-mamba.log BUILD_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]} if [ $BUILD_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then python3 -c " import json, datetime from pathlib import Path Path('outputs').mkdir(exist_ok=True) event = { 'ts': datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z', 'phase': 'P1.T1.1', 'status': 'FAILURE', 'category': 'DOCKER_BUILD_FAIL', 'log_file': '/tmp/docker-build-mamba.log', 'message': 'Docker build exited non-zero', } with open('outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl', 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f: f.write(json.dumps(event) + '\n') print('Diagnosis written to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl') " exit 1 fi

# FIX FOR B5: Verify mamba-ssm .so files in runner stage (not just builder) docker run --rm ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest \ python3 -c " import mamba_ssm import pathlib so_path = pathlib.Path(mamba_ssm.__file__).parent print(f'mamba_ssm {mamba_ssm.__version__} found at: {so_path}') # Verify CUDA extension .so exists in runner (not just .py stub) so_files = list(so_path.rglob('*.so')) assert so_files, 'FATAL: No .so files found in runner stage — CUDA extension missing from COPY' print(f'CUDA .so files: {[str(s.name) for s in so_files[:3]]}') print('B5 VERIFIED: mamba-ssm CUDA extension present in runner stage') " 2>&1 || (echo "FATAL: mamba-ssm import failed in runner — COPY stage broken" && exit 1)

task.acceptance: Build exits 0. mamba_ssm importable in runner. CUDA .so files present. "B5 VERIFIED: mamba-ssm CUDA extension present in runner stage" in output.
task.failure-action: Write JSONL event (already done above). STOP ENTIRE PLAN. Investigate: did COPY --from=builder /app/.venv /app/.venv include the .so dir? Check Dockerfile.base builder stage: uv sync --extra mamba-ssm path.

task.name: Push to GHCR with timeout
task.description: FIX FOR H3: Wrap push in 5-min timeout to prevent indefinite hang on slow connection.

commands.cmd: timeout 300 make push-mamba 2>&1 | tee /tmp/docker-push-mamba.log PUSH_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]} if [ $PUSH_EXIT -eq 124 ]; then echo "FATAL: Docker push TIMED OUT after 5 minutes (slow connection or GHCR outage)" exit 1 elif [ $PUSH_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then echo "FATAL: Docker push FAILED (exit $PUSH_EXIT)" exit 1 fi

# Verify push succeeded (not just exit 0) docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest 2>&1 | \ python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Pushed: {len(d[\"layers\"])} layers')" \ || (echo "FATAL: Manifest not found after push" && exit 1)

task.acceptance: docker manifest inspect returns valid JSON with layer count. Push timestamp within last 10 min.

task.name: Log rebuild to counter file
task.description: FIX FOR H2: Track Docker rebuilds to enforce max-1-rebuild policy in P4.

commands.cmd: COUNTER_FILE=/tmp/minivess_rebuild_count CURRENT=$(cat $COUNTER_FILE 2>/dev/null || echo 0) echo $((CURRENT + 1)) > $COUNTER_FILE echo "Docker rebuild count: $(cat $COUNTER_FILE) / 1 (max)"

task.name: Verify Docker image exists in registry

commands.cmd: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest 2>&1 | \ python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Image OK: {len(d[\"layers\"])} layers')" \ || (echo "FATAL: Image missing from GHCR" && exit 1)

task.name: Verify Network Volume exists and is reachable
task.description: FIX FOR H1: Explicit volume check, not just existence — confirms RunPod can mount it.

commands.cmd: uv run sky storage ls 2>&1 | grep minivess-dev \ || (echo "FATAL: minivess-dev volume missing — run P0.T0.3 first" && exit 1)

task.acceptance: sky storage ls shows minivess-dev. Volume state = available.

task.name: Validate smoke_test_mamba.yaml — strict checks
task.description: FIX FOR M4 + H8: Hard fail if YAML missing. Check UpCloud hostname not just var name. FIX FOR M5: volumes: (correct for NV) vs file_mounts: (wrong for NV).

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' from pathlib import Path import yaml, sys
yaml_path = Path("deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml") # M4 fix: hard fail if file missing (not silent pass) if not yaml_path.exists(): print("FATAL: smoke_test_mamba.yaml NOT FOUND") sys.exit(1)
cfg = yaml.safe_load(yaml_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) envs = cfg.get("envs", {}) setup = cfg.get("setup", "") run_block = cfg.get("run", "") resources = cfg.get("resources", {}) volumes = cfg.get("volumes", {})
failures = []
# Architecture checks if envs.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI") != "/opt/vol/mlruns": failures.append(f"MLflow must be file-based /opt/vol/mlruns, got: {envs.get('MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI')}")
# H8 fix: check UpCloud HOSTNAME not just var name presence upcloud_hostname = "pgopc.upcloudobjects.com" if upcloud_hostname in str(envs) or upcloud_hostname in setup: failures.append(f"UpCloud hostname found — UpCloud must be absent from RunPod YAML") if "MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME" in envs: failures.append("UpCloud MLflow auth var must NOT be present")
# Docker image check image_id = resources.get("image_id", "") if not image_id.startswith("docker:"): failures.append(f"image_id must start with docker:, got: {image_id}")
# M5 fix: Network Volume uses volumes: key (NOT file_mounts:) if not volumes: failures.append("volumes: block missing — Network Volume must be mounted via volumes: key") nv_found = any("/opt/vol" in str(v) for v in volumes.values() if isinstance(v, dict)) if not nv_found and "/opt/vol" not in str(volumes): failures.append("Network Volume at /opt/vol not found in volumes: block")
# Setup must handle missing data gracefully (check for fallback) if "s3://minivessdataset" not in setup and "/opt/vol/data/raw" not in setup: failures.append("setup: must check /opt/vol data OR fall back to s3://minivessdataset")
# EXPERIMENT must be computed in run: block, NOT envs (SkyPilot can't resolve intra-envs ${}) if "EXPERIMENT" in envs: failures.append("EXPERIMENT must NOT be in envs: block — compute in run: block only")
# run: block must use sky jobs launch convention (no sky launch) if "sky launch" in run_block and "sky jobs launch" not in run_block: failures.append("run: block must use sky jobs launch (spot recovery), not sky launch")
if failures: for f in failures: print(f"FAIL: {f}") sys.exit(1)
print("YAML pre-flight: ALL CHECKS PASSED") PYEOF

task.acceptance: ALL CHECKS PASSED. No FAIL lines.

task.name: Validate AMP policy — hard fail if cloud experiment config missing
task.description: FIX FOR M5: WARNING becomes hard failure. If cloud config missing, stop — don't use unknown Hydra defaults.

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' from pathlib import Path import yaml, sys
cloud_config = Path("configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet.yaml") if not cloud_config.exists(): print(f"FATAL: {cloud_config} missing — cannot verify AMP policy. Create it before overnight run.") sys.exit(1)
cfg = yaml.safe_load(cloud_config.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if cfg.get("mixed_precision") is not True: print(f"FAIL: mixed_precision must be True (train AMP ON), got: {cfg.get('mixed_precision')}") sys.exit(1) if cfg.get("mixed_precision_val") is not False: print(f"FAIL: mixed_precision_val must be False (val AMP OFF, MONAI #4243), got: {cfg.get('mixed_precision_val')}") sys.exit(1) print(f"AMP policy OK: train=ON val=OFF ({cloud_config})") PYEOF

task.acceptance: AMP policy OK. Hard fail on missing or incorrect config (no WARNING fallback).

task.name: Write VRAM sentinel check to /tmp
task.description: Verify smoke_test_mamba.yaml run: block emits VRAM_PEAK_MB sentinel (needed by P5.T5.1).

commands.cmd: python3 -c " from pathlib import Path run_block = Path('deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml').read_text(encoding='utf-8') _, _, after = run_block.partition('run: |') if 'VRAM_PEAK_MB=' not in after: print('FATAL: VRAM_PEAK_MB= sentinel missing from run: block in smoke_test_mamba.yaml') raise SystemExit(1) print('OK: VRAM_PEAK_MB sentinel present in run: block') "

task.acceptance: Sentinel present. If missing: add to smoke_test_mamba.yaml run: block before overnight launch.

task.name: Initialize cost tracking + launch smoke test

commands.cmd:
# Initialize cost tracking for this attempt ATTEMPT_START=$(date +%s) CUMULATIVE_COST=${CUMULATIVE_COST_USD:-0} echo "Attempt start: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ). Cumulative cost so far: \$${CUMULATIVE_COST}"
# M3 fix: check budget before each launch python3 -c " budget = 2.00 spent = float('${CUMULATIVE_COST}') remaining = budget - spent if remaining < 0.20: print(f'ABORT: Only \${remaining:.2f} remaining (min \$0.20 needed for attempt)') raise SystemExit(1) print(f'Budget OK: \${remaining:.2f} remaining') " # Launch (make smoke-test-mamba uses sky jobs launch — correct for spot recovery) set -a && source .env && set +a make smoke-test-mamba 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ralph-launch-mamba.log

task.acceptance: Job ID returned. sky jobs queue shows job PENDING or RUNNING.
task.on-failure: Capture log. Emit JSONL event. Classify failure. Go to P4.

task.name: Monitor to completion — polling loop with hard timeout
task.description: FIX FOR B3 + H4: Replace --no-follow (returns immediately) with polling loop. Hard timeout: 15 min. On timeout: classify as JOB_HANGING and trigger P4. Success condition: "Job SUCCEEDED" OR VRAM_PEAK_MB in logs (whichever comes first). Also handles: "Job FAILED" → immediate P4 trigger (don't wait for timeout).

commands.cmd:
JOB_ID=$(uv run sky jobs queue 2>&1 | python3 -c " import sys for line in sys.stdin: parts = line.split() if len(parts) >= 3 and 'mamba' in line.lower(): print(parts[0]) break " 2>/dev/null)
if [ -z "$JOB_ID" ]; then echo "FATAL: Could not find job ID in sky jobs queue" exit 1 fi echo "Monitoring job: $JOB_ID"
# Polling loop — FIX FOR B3 TIMEOUT=900 # 15 min hard timeout ELAPSED=0 STATUS="UNKNOWN" while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT ]; do LOGS=$(uv run sky jobs logs "$JOB_ID" 2>&1 || echo "LOG_FETCH_ERROR") echo "$LOGS" >> /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt
if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "Job SUCCEEDED\|Training complete\|VRAM_PEAK_MB="; then STATUS="SUCCEEDED" break fi if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "Job FAILED\|FATAL:\|Error:.*Training"; then STATUS="FAILED" break fi # Sky controller crash detection if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "LOG_FETCH_ERROR"; then echo "WARNING: Log fetch failed — controller may be restarting. Waiting..." fi
sleep 30 ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + 30)) echo "Elapsed: ${ELAPSED}s / ${TIMEOUT}s" done
if [ "$STATUS" = "UNKNOWN" ]; then STATUS="JOB_HANGING" echo "TIMEOUT: Job did not complete in ${TIMEOUT}s — classifying as JOB_HANGING" fi
echo "JOB_STATUS=$STATUS" > /tmp/ralph-mamba-status.txt echo "Final status: $STATUS" [ "$STATUS" = "SUCCEEDED" ] || exit 1

task.acceptance: JOB_STATUS=SUCCEEDED. /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt contains VRAM_PEAK_MB. Polling loop ran at 30s intervals with 15-min hard timeout.

task.name: Diagnose failure + write JSONL + update cost tracking
task.description: FIX FOR H5 + M3: Structured diagnosis before any retry. Track cumulative cost. Read /tmp/ralph-mamba-status.txt and /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt. Write event to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl. Check budget before proceeding to retry.

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' import json, os, datetime, sys from pathlib import Path
logs = Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8") if Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt").exists() else "" status_line = Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-status.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip() if Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-status.txt").exists() else "JOB_STATUS=UNKNOWN" job_status = status_line.partition("=")[2]
# Failure classification (str.partition — no regex per CLAUDE.md Rule #16) category = "UNKNOWN_FAILURE" if "unauthorized" in logs: category = "DOCKER_AUTH_FAIL" elif "insufficient-capacity" in logs or "sold out" in logs.lower(): category = "GPU_SOLD_OUT" elif "CUDA out of memory" in logs: category = "OOM_CUDA" elif "No training data" in logs: category = "DATA_MISSING" elif job_status == "JOB_HANGING": category = "JOB_HANGING" elif "ImportError" in logs and "mamba" in logs.lower(): category = "MAMBA_IMPORT_FAIL" elif "val_loss=nan" in logs.lower() or "nan" in logs.lower(): category = "NAN_VAL_LOSS" elif "No space left" in logs: category = "DISK_FULL"
# Cost tracking attempt_cost = float(os.environ.get("ATTEMPT_COST_USD", "0.15")) cumulative = float(os.environ.get("CUMULATIVE_COST_USD", "0")) + attempt_cost os.environ["CUMULATIVE_COST_USD"] = str(cumulative)
# H2 fix: check rebuild counter rebuild_count = int(Path("/tmp/minivess_rebuild_count").read_text() if Path("/tmp/minivess_rebuild_count").exists() else "0")
event = { "ts": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z", "phase": "P4.DIAGNOSE", "job_status": job_status, "category": category, "cumulative_cost_usd": round(cumulative, 3), "budget_remaining_usd": round(2.00 - cumulative, 3), "docker_rebuilds_used": rebuild_count, "auto_fixable": category in {"GPU_SOLD_OUT", "DATA_MISSING", "DISK_FULL", "OOM_CUDA"}, } Path("outputs").mkdir(exist_ok=True) with open("outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl", "a", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(json.dumps(event) + "\n")
print(f"Diagnosis: {category}") print(f"Cost: \${cumulative:.3f} / \$2.00 budget ({round((cumulative/2.00)*100, 1)}% used)") print(f"Rebuilds: {rebuild_count}/1")
# Budget gate if cumulative > 2.00: print("ABORT: Budget exceeded") sys.exit(99)
# Rebuild gate if category == "MAMBA_IMPORT_FAIL" and rebuild_count >= 1: print("ABORT: Second Docker rebuild would be needed — too complex for autonomous fix") sys.exit(98)
sys.exit(0) PYEOF

task.acceptance: JSONL event written. Budget OK. Rebuild count OK. Category classified.
task.abort-conditions: Exit 99: budget exceeded → go to P6 (write GitHub issue + exit) Exit 98: 2nd rebuild needed → go to P6 (write GitHub issue + exit) Exit 0: auto-fixable or retriable → continue to T4.2

task.name: Apply fix based on category

known-failure-patterns.pattern: SkyPilot auto-tries RTX4090 → RTX5090 → RTX3090 across regions. No action needed — retry T3.1 directly.
Reduce batch_size to 1 in experiment config. Edit: configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet.yaml → batch_size: 1. No rebuild needed.
Verify mixed_precision_val=false in cloud experiment config. Check: configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet.yaml. No rebuild needed.

smoke_test_mamba.yaml setup already falls back to s3://minivessdataset. If both fail: Network Volume data issue — retry may solve transient error. If persistent: create GitHub issue (network/bucket problem beyond autonomous fix).
Cancel stuck job: sky jobs cancel <job-id> -y Wait 60s for cleanup. Retry from T3.1. If 3rd hang: create GitHub issue.
GHCR package must be public for RunPod pull. gh api -X PATCH repos/petteriteikari/minivess-mlops/packages/container/minivess-base \ -f visibility=public Retry launch.

TDD: write failing test → fix COPY in Dockerfile.base → make build-mamba (if rebuild count 0). Check: did INSTALL_MAMBA=1 build arg pass through correctly? Max 1 rebuild — if 2nd would be needed, escalate to GitHub issue.
Increase disk_size in smoke_test_mamba.yaml (current: 40 GB). Change to 60 GB. No rebuild needed. Retry.

task.name: Extract VRAM — hard fail if missing
task.description: FIX FOR B4 + M1: Hard fail (exit 1) if VRAM_PEAK_MB not found. Also extract value for use in T5.3 commit message (no more [NNNN] placeholder).

commands.cmd:
VRAM_MB=$(python3 - <<'PYEOF' from pathlib import Path import sys
log_path = Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt") if not log_path.exists(): print("ERROR: Log file /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt not found", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(1)
log = log_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") for line in log.splitlines(): if "VRAM_PEAK_MB=" in line: _, _, val = line.partition("VRAM_PEAK_MB=") mb_str = val.strip().split()[0] mb = int(mb_str) print(mb) # stdout = the value break else: # FIX FOR B4: Hard fail, not WARNING print("FATAL: VRAM_PEAK_MB not found in logs — training may have crashed before measurement", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(1) PYEOF ) EXTRACT_EXIT=$? if [ $EXTRACT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then echo "FATAL: VRAM extraction failed — triggering P4 diagnosis" # Write failure event python3 -c " import json, datetime from pathlib import Path Path('outputs').mkdir(exist_ok=True) event = {'ts': datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()+'Z', 'phase': 'P5.T5.1', 'status': 'FAILURE', 'category': 'VRAM_MISSING'} open('outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl', 'a', encoding='utf-8').write(json.dumps(event)+'\n') " exit 1 fi echo "T09 VRAM: ${VRAM_MB} MB ($(python3 -c "print(f'{int(\"${VRAM_MB}\")/1024:.1f}')") GB)" # Export for T5.2 and T5.3 export VRAM_MB

task.acceptance: VRAM_MB is a valid integer. No "FATAL" output. Value exported for T5.2+T5.3.

task.name: TDD: RED→GREEN for VRAM profile update
task.description: FIX FOR H7 + H9 (TDD reviewer): Write RED test FIRST, then update YAML, then verify GREEN. Tests that mambavesselnet profile has measured=false (RED) then measured=true with ISO8601 date (GREEN). Uses self-learning-iterative-coder skill for this TDD loop.

tdd-spec.red:
Write test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py (tests/v2/unit/deployment/): test_mambavesselnet_vram_not_yet_measured: Load configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml with yaml.safe_load(). assert cfg["vram"]["measured"] is False, "Should fail before T5.2 update" This test MUST FAIL before the YAML is updated.
test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile_structure_after_update: Load configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml. # These assertions target the POST-update state: assert cfg["vram"]["measured"] is True assert isinstance(cfg["vram"].get("training_gb"), float) assert isinstance(cfg["vram"].get("measured_gpu"), str) # H9 fix: ISO8601 format validation (str.partition not regex) measured_date = cfg["vram"].get("measured_date", "") parts = measured_date.split("-") assert len(parts) == 3 and all(p.isdigit() for p in parts), \ f"measured_date must be YYYY-MM-DD, got: {measured_date}" assert int(parts[0]) >= 2026, "Year must be 2026 or later"
tdd-spec.green: Update configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml: vram: measured: true training_gb: (VRAM_MB / 1024, 1 decimal) measured_gpu: "RTX 4090" measured_date: "2026-03-16" patch_size: [64, 64, 64] batch_size: 1 amp: {train: true, val: false} Run: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py -v test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile_structure_after_update must PASS. test_mambavesselnet_vram_not_yet_measured must now FAIL (profile is updated) — expected.
tdd-spec.verify: make test-staging passes (no regressions). uv run ruff check src/ tests/ — no lint errors on new test file.

task.name: Commit + close #744 + create PR (with VRAM substituted)
task.description: FIX FOR M1: Substitute actual VRAM_MB into commit message (no [NNNN] placeholder). FIX FOR H6: git push MUST succeed before gh pr create (|| exit 1 guard).

commands.cmd: git add configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml \ tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py

git commit -m "$(cat <<EOF feat(mambavesselnet): T09 VRAM measured on RTX 4090, closes #744
VRAM peak: ${VRAM_MB} MB ($(python3 -c "print(f'{int(\"${VRAM_MB}\")/1024:.1f}')") GB) @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1 AMP: train=ON val=OFF (D04, MONAI #4243) GPU: RTX 4090 (24 GB, Ampere, BF16 supported) MLflow: /opt/vol/mlruns (Network Volume, sync via make dev-gpu-sync)
Tests: test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py (RED→GREEN TDD) Branch: test/mambavesselnet
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com> EOF )"
# FIX FOR H6: push MUST succeed before PR creation git push -u origin test/mambavesselnet \ || (echo "FATAL: git push failed — cannot create PR" && exit 1)

gh issue close 744 --comment "T09 VRAM measured + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod RTX 4090. VRAM peak: ${VRAM_MB} MB ($(python3 -c "print(f'{int(\"${VRAM_MB}\")/1024:.1f}')") GB) @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1. AMP: train=ON val=OFF (MONAI #4243). MLflow stored on /opt/vol/mlruns. Sync: make dev-gpu-sync. Branch: test/mambavesselnet → see PR below."

PR_URL=$(gh pr create \ --title "feat(mambavesselnet): T09-T10 VRAM measured + smoke test on RunPod RTX 4090" \ --body "$(cat <<'PRBODY' ## Summary
- T09: VRAM peak measured on RTX 4090 @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1 — **VRAM_MB_PLACEHOLDER MB** - T10: MambaVesselNet++ smoke test PASSED on RunPod (2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 vols, finite val_loss) - AMP: train=ON, val=OFF (D04, MONAI #4243 fix) - MLflow: file-based on Network Volume (/opt/vol/mlruns), sync via \`make dev-gpu-sync\` - TDD: test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py — RED→GREEN cycle completed - Closes #744
## Test plan
- [x] Smoke test SUCCEEDED on RunPod RTX 4090 - [x] VRAM_PEAK_MB captured in logs + committed to model profile YAML - [x] val_loss finite (AMP val OFF — D04) - [x] TDD: test_mambavesselnet_vram_profile.py GREEN + make test-staging passes - [x] ISO8601 measured_date, typed measured_gpu field
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) overnight autonomous run PRBODY )" \ --base main) echo "PR created: $PR_URL"

task.acceptance: Issue #744 closed. PR created with URL. Branch pushed to origin. No [NNNN] in commit message.

task.name: Write terminal JSONL event + create GitHub issue on failure

commands.cmd:
python3 - <<'PYEOF' import json, os, datetime, subprocess from pathlib import Path
# Determine overall status from ralph_diagnoses.jsonl diag_path = Path("outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl") last_status = "UNKNOWN" if diag_path.exists(): lines = [l for l in diag_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() if l.strip()] if lines: last_event = json.loads(lines[-1]) last_status = last_event.get("status", last_event.get("job_status", "UNKNOWN"))
vram = os.environ.get("VRAM_MB", "UNKNOWN") cumulative = float(os.environ.get("CUMULATIVE_COST_USD", "0"))
terminal_event = { "ts": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z", "phase": "TERMINAL", "status": "SUCCESS" if "SUCCEEDED" in last_status else "FAILURE", "vram_mb": vram, "cumulative_cost_usd": round(cumulative, 3), "budget_remaining_usd": round(2.00 - cumulative, 3), } diag_path.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True) with open(str(diag_path), "a", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(json.dumps(terminal_event) + "\n")
print(f"=== TERMINAL REPORT ===") print(f"Status: {terminal_event['status']}") print(f"VRAM: {vram} MB") print(f"Cost: \${terminal_event['cumulative_cost_usd']:.3f} / \$2.00") print(f"Log: outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl")
# If FAILURE: create GitHub issue (unless budget was the reason) if terminal_event["status"] == "FAILURE" and cumulative < 2.00: log_excerpt = "" if Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt").exists(): log_lines = Path("/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() log_excerpt = "\n".join(log_lines[-20:]) # last 20 lines
issue_body = f"""
## Summary
MambaVesselNet overnight smoke test FAILED autonomously on RunPod. Human intervention required.
## Diagnosis
Last event: {json.dumps(last_event if 'last_event' in dir() else terminal_event, indent=2)}
## Log excerpt (last 20 lines)
\`\`\` {log_excerpt} \`\`\`
## Cost incurred
\${cumulative:.3f} of \$2.00 budget used.
## References
- [`outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl`](outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl) """ result = subprocess.run( ["gh", "issue", "create", "--title", f"P0: MambaVesselNet overnight smoke FAILED — manual investigation needed", "--label", "P0-critical", "--body", issue_body], capture_output=True, text=True ) if result.returncode == 0: print(f"GitHub issue created: {result.stdout.strip()}") else: print(f"Warning: Could not create GitHub issue: {result.stderr.strip()}")
print("=== END REPORT (clean exit) ===") PYEOF

task.acceptance: Terminal JSONL event written. Report printed. GitHub issue created on failure. Exit 0 always.

close.reason: T09 VRAM measured + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod (Phase 5)

cost-model.gpu: RTX 4090 / RTX 5090 / RTX 3090 (all ≥24 GB, BF16 supported)

before-launching.note: Human verifies once, then goes offline
before-launching.check: set -a && source .env && set +a (exports all vars to subprocesses)
uv run sky status (confirm controller at EU-CZ-1)
Make GHCR package public if not already (see P0.T0.2)

execution-checklist.overnight-command: # The overnight-runner skill invokes this plan: /overnight-runner --config docs/planning/overnight-mambavesselnet-v2.yaml # Which runs: python3 scripts/overnight_runner.py docs/planning/mambavesselnet-overnight-optimized.xml
execution-checklist.wake-up-check: cat outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl | python3 -c "import json,sys; [print(json.dumps(json.loads(l))) for l in sys.stdin if l.strip()]" | tail -5
